rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER PATTEE VS. PUBLIC RECREATION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Friday, March 07, 2025 Line 8. 7. MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. The matter is ordered off calendar. Discovery matters shall be heard by a discovery referee per 1/27/2025 order. If parties have yet to agree on the appointment, they shall meet and confer by 3/14/2025 to select the referee. If the parties cannot agree, the moving party shall file a motion for the selection of the referee and parties shall each submit 3 names for the court's consideration. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/BZ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Friday, March 07, 2025 Line 10 PLAINTIFF CORINTHIA CRISTOFANI's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two. 1. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two/Denied without prejudice to renew. 2. Plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.1345, which provides that "[m]aterial must not be incorporated into the separate statement by reference." (See Separate Statement 2:17-21.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: SUHAILA FARHAT VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action (Amended)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Friday, March 07, 2025 Line 11. DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action. (Amended) Uber Technologies, Inc Motion to Compel Arbitration and to stay the action, is denied. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/BZ)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLAS ROSARIO LAM VS. BRIGHT FITNESS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Coordinate Cases (Case No. 37-2024-00031047-Cu-Fr-Nc, San Diego)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Friday, March 07, 2025 Line 4. DEFENDANT NERISSA ZHANG's Motion To Coordinate Cases (Case No. 37-2024-00031047-Cu-Fr-Nc, San Diego) NERISSA ZHANG's Motion To Coordinate Cases, is denied. 1. Defendant Zhang seeks to have this Court send the instant case to San Diego and coordinate it with the parties' San Diego action (Zhang v. Lam, Case No. 37-2024-00031047-CU-FR-NC). 2. This Court lacks the authority to issue such an order. 3. Under CCP 403, "[a] judge may, on motion, transfer an action or actions from another court to that judge's court for coordination." (See also CRC 3.500(h).) 4. If the defendant wishes to have this action transferred to San Diego, the motion needs to be made in the San Diego action. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/BZ)
